req.title: Account-level transaction limits
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three key behaviours: (1) a valid limit update within regulatory caps stores a pending limit and activates after 2 hours (positive / EP), (2) a limit above the regulatory cap is rejected with HTTP 422 (negative / NEG), (3) a limit exactly at the regulatory cap boundary is accepted (boundary / BVA), (4) the state transition from 'pending' to 'active' after the cool-down elapses (STC), and (5) conflicting rule combinations via a decision table covering channel × cap combos (DT). This gives five tests, covering all three required kinds and all five heuristics.

tests.name: test_valid_limit_update_stores_pending_and_returns_202
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested daily limit is within the regulatory cap for the channel
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with a daily_limit well below the regulatory cap for channel 'online'
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an effective_at timestamp approximately 2 hours in the future
trace: R-FIN-017: customer shall configure daily per-channel limits
R-FIN-017: changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
def test_valid_limit_update_stores_pending_and_returns_202(auth_headers, account_id, base_url, regulatory_cap):
    import requests, datetime
    daily_limit = regulatory_cap("online") // 2
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": daily_limit}
    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    effective_at = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_at"])
    now = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
    delta_hours = (effective_at - now).total_seconds() / 3600
    assert 1.9 <= delta_hours <= 2.1, f"Cool-down window unexpected: {delta_hours}h"

tests.name: test_limit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422
preconditions: Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested daily limit exceeds the regulatory cap for the channel
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_limit set to regulatory_cap + 1 for channel 'online'
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an error code referencing the regulatory cap violation
trace: R-FIN-017: changes take effect within regulatory caps
tests.executable:
def test_limit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422(auth_headers, account_id, base_url, regulatory_cap):
    import requests
    over_cap = regulatory_cap("online") + 1
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": over_cap}
    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "regulatory_cap" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
           "cap" in body.get("detail", "").lower(), \
           f"Expected cap-related error, got: {body}"

tests.name: test_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted
preconditions: Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Requested daily limit is exactly equal to the regulatory cap for the channel
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_limit equal to regulatory_cap exactly for channel 'online'
Capture HTTP status code and response body
trace: R-FIN-017: changes take effect within regulatory caps (inclusive boundary)
tests.executable:
def test_limit_exactly_at_regulatory_cap_is_accepted(auth_headers, account_id, base_url, regulatory_cap):
    import requests
    cap = regulatory_cap("online")
    payload = {"channel": "online", "daily_limit": cap}
    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202 at cap boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending", f"Expected pending, got: {body['status']}"

tests.name: test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown
preconditions: Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
A pending limit update has been submitted (pre-seeded via test fixture)
System clock has been advanced past the 2-hour cool-down (time-travel fixture)
actions: Seed a pending limit via helper fixture with effective_at set to now - 1 second
GET /accounts/{id}/limits to read current effective limit
expected: Response body shows the previously pending limit is now status='active'
active daily_limit matches the seeded value
tests.executable:
def test_pending_limit_becomes_active_after_cooldown(
    auth_headers, account_id, base_url, seed_expired_pending_limit
):
    import requests
    seeded_limit = seed_expired_pending_limit(
        account_id=account_id,
        channel="online",
        daily_limit=5000,
        offset_seconds=-1,
    )
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    limits = resp.json().get("limits", [])
    online = next((l for l in limits if l["channel"] == "online"), None)
    assert online is not None, "No limit entry for channel 'online'"
    assert online["status"] == "active", f"Expected active, got: {online['status']}"
    assert online["daily_limit"] == seeded_limit, \
        f"Expected {seeded_limit}, got {online['daily_limit']}"

tests.name: test_decision_table_channel_cap_combinations
preconditions: Account ID exists and belongs to the authenticated customer
Regulatory caps differ per channel (online, atm, branch)
actions: For each channel in [online, atm, branch], submit daily_limit = regulatory_cap + 1
Collect HTTP status codes and error responses
expected: HTTP 422 for every over-cap submission across all channels
Each error response references the respective channel's regulatory cap
trace: R-FIN-017: per-channel limits within regulatory caps
